title: Auto-Fetch Dashboards on First Load of Metrics Analyst
story: As a DataTools Pro user opening the Metrics Analyst for the first time, I want dashboards to automatically start loading with a visual indicator, so that I don't have to manually trigger a refresh and can see the feature is working.
description: When the Metrics Analyst modal opens and no dashboards are available (because they've never been loaded), the system should automatically trigger a dashboard fetch from Salesforce. A loading indicator (three-dot animation) should signal that dashboards are being loaded.
acceptance_criteria: Opening Metrics Analyst with no cached dashboards automatically triggers a fetch
A three-dot loading animation displays while dashboards are being fetched
Once fetch completes, the dashboard list populates automatically
Subsequent opens use cached data without auto-fetching
ux_changes: Added automatic loading animation when Metrics Analyst opens with no dashboards. Shows a three-dot loading indicator during the Salesforce fetch.
middleware_changes: Added logic to detect empty dashboard state on modal open and auto-trigger the Salesforce dashboard sync.
implementation_notes: The refresh button was added first (MBT-507), then auto-loading on first open was added as a follow-up enhancement.
comment_summary: Developer added the refresh button first, then implemented auto-loading when no dashboards exist. Completed on staging.
